content:
Case 9:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 81
- Sex: male
- Chief complaint: New-onset swelling of the right shoulder and upper arm with localized pain and reduced range of motion
- Vital signs: BP 118/70 mmHg, HR 92/min (irregular), Temp 36.9 °C, RR 18/min, SpO₂ 95 % (room air)
Setting: Outpatient clinic (initial presentation with localized musculoskeletal swelling)
Capabilities: You are evaluating a clinically stable patient in an ambulatory hematology setting. You can obtain a detailed history and examination, request routine blood work, and arrange standard non-invasive imaging appropriate for an initial assessment. Invasive biopsy procedures and complex diagnostic evaluations require additional planning.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "physical examination of the right shoulder and upper arm"}}

response.request: physical examination of the right shoulder and upper arm

content:
History Physical:
2025-07-03: The patient presented to the hematology outpatient clinic with new-onset swelling of the right shoulder and upper arm, which developed over the past several weeks and was associated with localized pain and reduced range of motion. He also reported foamy urine, but denied constitutional B symptoms such as fever, night sweats, or unintentional weight loss. There was no recent history of infection or trauma. Physical examination revealed asymmetric swelling of the right shoulder and upper arm with mild tenderness to palpation. No overlying erythema or warmth was noted. No palpable cervical, axillary, or inguinal lymphadenopathy. Lungs were clear on auscultation; cardiac rhythm was irregularly irregular. The abdomen was soft and non-tender without hepatosplenomegaly. No peripheral edema was present. Neurological exam showed intact cranial nerves and normal gait.
Current medication: allopurinol 100 mg 1-0-0, calcium carbonate 500 mg 1-0-0, cotrimoxazole 480 mg 1-0-0 (Mon/Wed/Fri), tamsulosin 0.4 mg 0-0-1, ramipril 5 mg 1-0-0, torasemide 10 mg 0-1-0, valaciclovir 500 mg 1-0-0, ophthalmic timolol 0.5% (1 drop both eyes BID), atorvastatin 10 mg 0-0-1, apixaban 2.5 mg 1-0-1, bisoprolol 2.5 mg 1-0-1, and pregabalin 75 mg 1-0-1.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "complete blood count"}}

Cbc Coagulation:
CBC: Hb 10.4 g/dL, WBC 6.1 × 10⁹/L, Plt 104 × 10⁹/L; MCV 90 fL, MCH 29 pg. Differential: neutrophils 60 %, lymphocytes 28 %, monocytes 8 %, eosinophils 3 %, basophils 1 %.
Coagulation: aPTT 31 s, INR 1.6.

Chemistry: Na 134 mmol/L, K 5.1 mmol/L, creatinine 2.3 mg/dL (eGFR 26 mL/min/1.73 m²), BUN 48 mg/dL, AST 28 U/L, ALT 32 U/L, bilirubin 0.9 mg/dL, LDH 265 U/L, uric acid 8.2 mg/dL, Ca²⁺ 3.02 mmol/L, phosphate 4.9 mg/dL.
Immunology: IgG 1820 mg/dL, free κ light chains 412 mg/L, κ/λ ratio 48.
Urinalysis: protein +++ without hematuria.
NT-proBNP 2350 pg/mL.

2025-07-03: CT skeleton showed multiple osteolytic lesions of the axial and appendicular skeleton, most prominent in vertebrae, ribs, and pelvis, without signs of new destruction, consistent with inactive post-treatment myeloma lesions. Soft-tissue swelling at the right shoulder and proximal arm incompletely characterized due to lack of contrast.

{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "bone marrow biopsy with morphology, histology, and flow cytometry"}}

response.request: bone marrow biopsy with morphology, histology, and flow cytometry

content:
Bone Marrow:
Bone marrow smears were normocellular with approximately 5-10 % plasma cells. The plasma cells appeared large with eccentric nuclei, coarse “clock-face” chromatin, prominent nucleoli, and abundant basophilic cytoplasm. Occasional binucleated forms were noted. The background marrow showed mildly reduced granulopoiesis and dysmorphic erythropoiesis, with scattered small lymphocytes and occasional mast cells. No excess of blasts was identified. Flow cytometry revealed 0.9 % plasma cells with strong expression of CD38⁺, CD138⁺, and CD27⁺ and dim CD56 expression, showing cytoplasmic kappa light-chain restriction. CD19 and CD45 were negative. No aberrant myeloid or B-cell populations were detected, 1.2% CD34low blasts.
Histology (core biopsy) demonstrated age-adjusted normocellular marrow (overall cellularity 40 %) with nodular and interstitial plasma-cell infiltrates, accounting for 5-10 % of total marrow cellularity. Immunohistochemistry confirmed CD138⁺ plasma cells with kappa light-chain restriction, negative for lambda. Residual trilineage hematopoiesis was present but mildly reduced with dysplastic erythropoiesis.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "serum and urine protein electrophoresis with immunofixation"}}

response.request: serum and urine protein electrophoresis with immunofixation

content:
Protein Studies:
Urinalysis: proteinuria (++), leukocyturia (120/µL), positive for albumin; κ/λ free light-chain ratio 18.3.
Serum protein electrophoresis revealed a distinct monoclonal IgG κ band with an M-spike, confirmed by immunofixation. Urine electrophoresis demonstrated monoclonal κ light-chain proteinuria.
β₂-microglobulin 4.8 mg/L, serum albumin 3.2 g/dL.

content:
Pet Ct:
2025-07-06: FDG PET-CT: Demonstration of a focal FDG-avid lesion at the right shoulder and upper arm (SUVmax 8.2), correlating with the clinical swelling. Additional hypermetabolic foci were seen in the proximal humerus (SUVmax 6.5) and right scapula (SUVmax 5.9), compatible with active myeloma involvement. No other FDG-avid lesions were detected in the skeleton, visceral organs, or lymph nodes.

content: {"response": {"action": "request", "request": "detailed treatment history and cytogenetic/molecular risk profile"}}

response.request: detailed treatment history and cytogenetic/molecular risk profile

content:
Treatment History:
2013-03-01: Initial diagnosis of IgG κ multiple myeloma, without CRAB criteria. Staging: ISS II, Salmon & Durie II. Initial management: Watch & wait.
2014-09-01; Disease progression with anemia. Initiation of induction therapy with VCD for 3 cycles (q21d): Bortezomib 1.3 mg/m² s.c. (days 1, 4, 8, 11) , Cyclophosphamide 900 mg/m² i.v. (day 1), Dexamethasone 40 mg p.o. (days 1–4 and 9–12)
2015-09-01: Stem cell mobilization with CE regimen: Cyclophosphamide 2 g/m² i.v. (days 1–2), etoposide 100 mg/m² i.v. (days 1–3), followed by G-CSF 5 µg/kg/day from day 4 until stem cell collection.
2015-10-01: High-dose melphalan therapy 2 x 70 mg/m² i.v. (total 140 mg/m²), followed by autologous stem cell transplantation (ASCT). No maintenance therapy administered.
2017-01-01 Biochemical signs of relapse with slowly rising IgG (from 1300 → 1850 mg/dL over 12 months) and increasing κ free light chains (120 → 220 mg/L).
2018-02-04: Bone marrow biopsy: 20 % plasma cell infiltration. FISH: trisomy 3, t(4;14), 13q14 deletion, 1q21 gain, CDKN2A deletion. CT skeleton: no new osteolytic lesions.
2018-11-01: Slim-CRAB criteria met due to FLC ratio >100 (IgG 2312 mg/dL).
Echocardiogram: preserved systolic function (LVEF 58%), no valvular abnormalities. Worsening kidney function (creatinine 1.6 mg/dL). Start of KRD (q28d): Carfilzomib 20 mg/m² i.v. (days 1–2, cycle 1), then 27 mg/m² twice weekly (days 1–2, 8–9, 15–16). Lenalidomide 15 mg p.o. (days 1–21) Dexamethasone 40 mg weekly
2019-02-01: Serological complete remission (CR) achieved. No bone marrow reassessment performed.
2019-06-01: Second high-dose therapy with melphalan 2 x 70 mg/m² i.v. followed by ASCT.
Course complicated by onset of peripheral neuropathy and severe infectious episode (neutropenic fever requiring i.v. antibiotics) as well as acute kidney failure.
2021-04-01: Biochemical relapse: Immunofixation positive again with slowly rising IgG (1200 → 1650 mg/dL) and κ free light chains (84 → 187 mg/L).
2022-02-04. Start of Isa-Pom-Dex regimen: Isatuximab 10 mg/kg i.v. weekly x4, then every 2 weeks, Pomalidomide 3 mg p.o. (days 1–21), Dexamethasone 40 mg weekly: achieved VGPR.
2022-11-01: Lymphocyte apheresis performed in preparation for CAR-T therapy.
2023-01-30: CAR-T cell therapy with idecabtagene vicleucel following lymphodepletion with: Fludarabine 30 mg/m² i.v. (days –5 to –3, 50% dose reduction due to CKD stage 3), Cyclophosphamide 500 mg/m² i.v. (days –5 to –3). Infusion on 2023-01-30. Achieved VGPR, followed by CR on 2023-03-24.
2023-07-01 Cytopenia developed without evidence of therapy-related MDS or myeloma relapse.
2024-02-01 CT scan: new osteolytic lesions. IgG 1920 mg/dL and κ free light chains 191 mg/L. Immunofixation positive. Echocardiogram: LVEF 45%. Initiated ixazomib 4 mg (days 1, 8, 15) + dexamethasone 20 mg weekly, omitting IMiD due to mild cytopenia and CKD.
2024-10-01: Progressive cytopenia. Bone marrow: dysplastic changes with erythroid and myeloid atypia, no excess blasts. TP53 mutation (high VAF) detected in CD138-negative fraction; karyotyping unsuccessful. Ixazomib monotherapy continued briefly, but paused in December 2024 resulting in increased blood counts.
